name: Priti — 67, cares for her husband, earns a penny over the limit
note: The cliff edge: £204.01 of weekly earnings loses the whole allowance — no taper, no shortfall, nothing. The verdict names the cliff and the deductions worth checking before giving up. Her carer answer to Pension Credit is honestly no: over the limit there is no entitlement, underlying or otherwise.
checked: Earnings limit (£204, cliff-edge, allowable deductions) against gov.uk/carers-allowance/eligibility, 2026-07-12.

carers-allowance.because: Your earnings are over the £204 weekly limit — and it is a cliff edge: a penny over loses the whole allowance. Before giving up, check what counts: the test is after tax, National Insurance, half of any pension contributions, and some care costs while you work.
